reasoning: The O-DU is identified as the origin of the failure because it is the common junction for the E2, F1-c, and F1-u interfaces, and the root KPI is specific to the E2-DU uplink. Severe packet loss on the E2 interface Uplink (UL) originating from the O-DU caused a systemic failure that propagated through the control and user planes to the O-CU and the RIC hierarchy.
actionable_steps: 1. Inspect the O-DU network interface card (NIC) and physical cabling associated with the E2 interface. 2. Verify O-DU resource utilization (CPU/Memory) to ensure the E2 agent is not crashing or dropping packets due to overload. 3. Reset the E2 connection between the O-DU and the Near-RT RIC to clear any stale sessions or buffer overflows.
